SubagentStop.description: Ralph — auto-continue crew/officer agents that stop with questions instead of results
Auto-dispatch officers when crew agents complete implementation work matching SDLC concerns

hooks.prompt:
Determine if the crew agent performed implementation work (code changes). Look for evidence in its response: file paths it wrote/edited, commit messages, branch names, FLEET_NOTIFICATION blocks, or statements like 'created/modified/updated/refactored/fixed/implemented'. Mentions of 'reviewed', 'acknowledged', 'no action needed', or pure Q&A responses do NOT count as implementation work.

If NO implementation work was performed, do nothing — say nothing.

If implementation work WAS performed:
1. Identify the crew name from the response (look for crew name in branch names like fleet/crew/<name>/... or agent self-identification).
2. Read .fleet/crews/<crew-name>.yaml to get the sdlc_concerns list.
3. If sdlc_concerns is empty or missing, do nothing — say nothing.
4. Read all .fleet/officers/*.yaml files.
5. For each officer, check if ANY of the officer's triggers list items match ANY of the crew's sdlc_concerns items (exact string match).
6. For each matching officer, dispatch it via the Agent tool with this prompt:
   'You are the <officer title> (<officer name>).
   Your config:
   <full officer YAML contents>
   ---
   Auto-dispatched after <crew-name>-crew completed implementation work.
   Crew config: <crew YAML contents>
   Changes summary: <extract from crew response: what files were changed and what was done>
   ---
   Review the crew\'s changes against your review_checklist and expertise. Return structured findings with severity (critical/warning/info) and recommendations. Focus only on your domain. Be concise.'
7. After dispatching, briefly confirm: 'Fleet: auto-dispatched officer review — <officer-names> reviewing <crew-name>-crew changes.'

If .fleet/ directory does not exist, do nothing.

SubagentStop.description: Persist crew notification logs when a crew agent reports partner-impacting changes

hooks.prompt:
Check the crew agent's response for FLEET_NOTIFICATION blocks (HTML comments starting with <!-- FLEET_NOTIFICATION). If none are found, do nothing — say nothing. If one or more are found: 1) Ensure .fleet/changes/ directory exists (create it if not). 2) For each notification block, extract: crew, affected_partners, severity, summary, detail. 3) Write a changelog entry to .fleet/changes/YYYY-MM-DD-<crew>-<short-slug>.md with this format:
---
date: YYYY-MM-DD
crew: <crew>
affected_partners: [<partners>]
severity: <severity>
acknowledged_by: []
---

<detail text>

4) After writing, briefly confirm: 'Fleet: logged <severity> notification from <crew>-crew affecting <partners>.' 5) If severity is breaking or major, dispatch each affected partner's crew agent with: 'You are the <partner>-crew agent. --- Notification from <crew>-crew [<severity>]: <summary>. <detail> --- Review this notification. If action is needed on your owned paths, describe what should change. If no action needed, acknowledge the change.' For minor/info severity, skip dispatch — the changelog entry is sufficient.

PostToolUse.description: Check if edited file is owned by a crew and suggest partner notifications

hooks.prompt: Check if the file that was just written/edited falls within any crew's owned paths. To do this: 1) Read .fleet/crews/*.yaml files to get all crews' paths. 2) Check if the edited file matches any crew's path globs. If it matches a crew AND the change could affect partner crews (based on the crew's partners list), suggest: 'This file is owned by <crew-name>-crew. Consider notifying partners: <partner-list>. Run /fleet notify <partner> <summary> to notify.' If the file is not owned by any crew, note: 'This file is not covered by any Fleet crew.' Be brief — one line if no action needed. If .fleet/ directory does not exist, say nothing.

SessionStart.description: Load fleet context, surface unacknowledged changes, and detect pending bridge work

hooks.prompt:
If .fleet/bridge.yaml does not exist, say nothing. Otherwise:

1. Silently read .fleet/bridge.yaml and all .fleet/crews/*.yaml to understand the fleet structure.

2. Check .fleet/changes/ for entries from the last 24 hours where affected_partners contains crews not yet in acknowledged_by.

3. Run: git branch --list 'fleet/crew/*' 'fleet/review/*' 2>/dev/null — to find pending crew branches.

4. Check version drift: read fleet_plugin_version from .fleet/bridge.yaml. Read ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/.claude-plugin/plugin.json for current plugin version. If they differ (or fleet_plugin_version is missing), flag it.

5. Build a Fleet status block with only the items that apply (skip items with nothing to report):
   - If version drift: 'Fleet plugin updated (<old> → <new>). Run /fleet upgrade to sync.'
   - If unacknowledged changes: 'X unacknowledged change(s). Run /fleet changelog.'
   - If crew/review branches exist: 'N branch(es) pending merge. Run /fleet bridge release.'
   - If .fleet/officers/ directory is empty or missing while crews have sdlc_concerns: 'No officers defined. Run /fleet officer create.'

6. If any items found, output them prefixed with 'Fleet:' on separate lines. If nothing to report, say nothing.

Be brief — one line per item, no extra formatting.
